Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Key roles: Accounts Payable Clerk (Mary), Purchasing Officer (Dan), Department Manager (Carlos), AP Supervisor (Rita), Supplier Relationship Manager (Karen). Also suppliers. Process starts when invoice arrives (usually by email). Mary checks if PO number associated. If no PO, send back to supplier for more details (Mary). If PO exists, log into accounting system. Then forward to purchasing (Dan) to confirm goods/services received.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Dan checks invoice vs PO. If missing PO number, Dan tells Mary to get info (but thats earlier step? Actually Mary already checked PO, so Dans comment about missing PO might be rare? He says if the PO number is missing, I tell Mary she needs to get that information before I can proceed. That could be a rework loop. But Marys step already checks PO. Possibly some invoices arrive without PO but then get PO later? Well handle.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After Dan checks, if everything correct, sends confirmation email back to AP. If discrepancy, either contact supplier directly or ask AP to hold invoice until supplier sends corrected version. If discrepancy cant be resolved easily, Mary escalates to Rita (supervisor). Rita tries to resolve, possibly involving Karen (supplier relationship manager) for pricing or missing PO. After resolution, invoice goes back to normal flow: confirmation from Purchasing, then manager approval.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Manager Carlos gets notification after matching and Dans confirmation. He approves or rejects (or asks hold). If rejected, then investigation? He says reject or ask Mary to hold and investigate. That might lead back to resolution. After approval, Mary schedules for payment (payment run).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to model sequence flows and gateways. Lets produce textual BPMN description. Use standard BPMN elements: start event, tasks, exclusive gateways, parallel gateways if any, end events. Well describe pools/lanes for roles. Output should be clear enough to convert to diagram. Ill write in a structured format, perhaps using indentation or bullet points, with roles and gateways noted. Ill produce a model with start event: Invoice received (by email).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 1. AP Clerk checks if invoice has a valid PO number. (Task: Check PO Validity) - Gateway: Has PO? (Exclusive) If NO: AP Clerk sends invoice back to Supplier requesting PO details. Supplier sends revised invoice or PO number. After receiving, go back to Step 1 (loop). If YES: Proceed to Step 2. 2. AP Clerk logs invoice into accounting system. 3. AP Clerk forwards invoice to Purchasing for confirmation. 4. Purchasing checks invoice details against Purchase Order (PO). Gateway: Are there discrepancies? If NO discrepancies: Purchasing sends confirmation email to AP Clerk. Proceed to Step 5. If YES (discrepancies): Sub-gateway: Can discrepancy be resolved easily? If YES: Purchasing contacts supplier for correction. Supplier sends corrected invoice. Loop back to Step 4. If NO: AP Clerk escalates to AP Supervisor. AP Supervisor investigates and coordinates with Supplier Relationship Manager if needed. After resolution, invoice returns to normal flow.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 5. After receiving confirmation from Purchasing, AP Clerk (or system) triggers manager approval. 6. Department Manager reviews invoice details, PO, and confirmation. Gateway: Approve or Reject? If APPROVE: Manager approves in system. Proceed to Step 7. If REJECT: Manager rejects or asks AP Clerk to hold and investigate. Leads back to resolution (loop). 7. After manager approval, AP Clerk schedules invoice for payment. 8. End Event: Invoice paid in next payment cycle.

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Note: There is also a possibility of missing PO number at this stage (Dans comment). If Purchasing finds PO missing, they ask AP Clerk to get info. This would be a rework. Ambiguities / Uncertainties: Exact handling of missing PO at Purchasing stage is not fully clear; it might be rare. Whether manager rejection leads to a formal rework loop or just stops the process is not specified.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Exceptions / Variations (non-standard paths): Invoices without any PO handled by AP Supervisor working with department heads to create retrospective PO or reject. Some invoices may have discrepancies that require Supplier Relationship Manager to resolve pricing issues. Rework loops are present at multiple points. Data Artifacts: Invoice (initial), Purchase Order (PO), Goods receipt confirmation, Approval notification (email), Payment schedule.
